2605.29283.title: Do Physics Foundation Models Learn Generalizable Physics? A Bias-Aware Benchmark Across Physical Regimes and Distribution Shifts
2605.29283.paper_summary: Recent physics foundation models claim general spatiotemporal forecasting ability, yet their evaluations often collapse performance into a single average score under a fixed training distribution. This makes it difficult to determine whether a model has learned generalizable physical dynamics or only performs well under particular settings. We construct a benchmark with 8 physical dynamics, 3 training-data mixtures, and 25 test regimes induced by dynamic-scale and initial-condition complexity shifts, covering in-distribution, distribution-shift, and out-of-distribution settings. We evaluate five physics foundation model architectures and four model variants per architecture (scratch and three pretrained sizes), resulting in 60,000 measurements. Our results show that current physics foundation models behave as conditional rather than universal generalists: their generality depends on the physical regime, temporal scale, initial-condition setting, pretraining, model size, and architecture. Improving the training data distribution only partially mitigates this limitation. Pretraining and scaling are also unable to reliably remove their ability biases. We argue that improving physics foundation models requires moving beyond scaling models or expanding data, toward learning mechanisms that better capture transferable physical knowledge across regimes, temporal scales, and distribution shifts.
2605.29283.paper_summary_zh: 最近的物理基礎模型聲稱具有一般的時空預測能力，然而它們的評估常常將性能壓縮為在固定訓練分佈下的單一平均分數。這使得難以確定模型是否學會了可泛化的物理動力學，或者僅僅是在特定設置下表現良好。我們構建了一個基準，包含 8 種物理動力學、3 種訓練數據混合以及 25 種由動態尺度和初始條件複雜度變化引起的測試方案，涵蓋了內部分佈、分佈轉移和外部分佈設置。我們評估了五種物理基礎模型架構以及每種架構的四種模型變體（從頭開始和三種預訓練大小），總共進行了 60,000 次測量。我們的結果顯示，當前的物理基礎模型表現為條件性而非普遍性通才：它們的普遍性依賴於物理範疇、時間尺度、初始條件設置、預訓練、模型大小和架構。改善訓練數據分佈僅部分減輕了這一限制。預訓練和擴展也無法可靠地消除它們的能力偏差。我們認為，改善物理基礎模型需要超越擴展模型或擴大數據，朝著學習更好捕捉跨範疇、時間尺度和分佈轉移的可轉移物理知識的機制邁進。
2605.29283.author: Mengdi Chu et.al.
2605.29283.authors: Mengdi Chu, Yang Liu, Ayan Biswas, Han-Wei Shen
